2606.07714.title: Beyond Accuracy: Interpreting Topic Representation in Suicide Ideation Detection Models
2606.07714.paper_summary: Suicide ideation detection models are typically evaluated using aggregate performance metrics, yet little is known about how they internally represent psychologically meaningful risk factors. In high-stakes mental health applications, understanding these internal representations is essential for safety, transparency, and responsible deployment. In this work, we move beyond accuracy and analyze how suicide detection models trained on original and topic-augmented datasets encode psychological risk factors in their internal representation space. Using visualization and geometric analysis, we examine the coherence and separability of topic-related features. Our results show that topic-aware augmentation increases the clarity and distinctness of underrepresented psychosocial risk factors such as immigration, family issues, and financial crisis. These findings suggest that augmentation not only improves model performance but also leads to more structured and interpretable internal representations.
2606.07714.paper_summary_zh: 自殺意念檢測模型通常使用綜合性能指標進行評估，但對於它們如何在內部表示心理上有意義的風險因素知之甚少。在高風險的心理健康應用中，理解這些內部表示對於安全性、透明度和負責任的部署至關重要。在本研究中，我們超越準確性，分析在原始和主題增強數據集上訓練的自殺檢測模型如何在其內部表示空間中編碼心理風險因素。通過可視化和幾何分析，我們檢查主題相關特徵的連貫性和可分性。我們的結果顯示，主題感知增強提高了移民、家庭問題和財務危機等被低估的心理社會風險因素的清晰度和獨特性。這些發現表明，增強不僅改善了模型性能，還導致了更具結構性和可解釋性的內部表示。
2606.07714.author: Hamideh Ghanadian et.al.
2606.07714.authors: Hamideh Ghanadian, Isar Nejadgholi, Hussein Al Osman
